title: Canonical feature pruning to reduce duplicate signals
summary: Prune redundant aliases and keep one canonical version of each race-state, finish-window, option-count, and rule-state signal.
rationale: The run produced many exact or near-identical submissions from feature variants, indicating that redundant features often did not change predictions. A compact canonical stack can improve runtime, attribution, and ensemble diversity.
implementation_hint: Keep one naming convention and one or two ratios per concept: current finish margin, next-stop option count, window-opening count, prior pit debt, slick-diversity debt, and core raw transforms. Compare prediction correlation and validation behavior against a larger feature stack.
expected_effect: Small stability and runtime improvement with medium-high confidence; AUC may stay similar while reducing overfit.
risk: Pruning can remove a weak but useful interaction feature. Use an ablation table rather than pruning all variants at once.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
